title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with our easy recipe. This hearty, flavorful Middle Eastern soup is perfect for any meal!

seo.h1: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas?
Step-by-Step Instructions for Lebanese Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: A comforting bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a staple of Middle Eastern cuisine.

description: Experience the comforting warmth of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, a hearty and flavorful staple of Middle Eastern cuisine. This simple yet nourishing soup is packed with wholesome ingredients, offering a delightful blend of earthy lentils, aromatic spices, and a bright lemon finish. Perfect for a light meal or a comforting appetizer, Adas is a beloved dish cherished across Lebanese homes.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Vegetables
instructions.text: Begin by thoroughly rinsing your red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain them well. Peel and finely chop the yellow onion, mince the garlic cloves, and dice the carrot and potato into small, uniform pieces. This preparation ensures even cooking.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes, until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables, Lentils, and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the diced carrot and potato, cooking for about 3-5 minutes until they start to soften slightly. Add the rinsed red lentils, ground cumin, and turmeric powder (if using). Stir everything together for about a minute, allowing the spices to become fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Adjust Consistency and Season
instructions.text: Once the lentils and vegetables are tender, remove the pot from the heat. You can leave the soup chunky or use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend it to your desired consistency. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh lemon juice.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley. Serve immediately with extra lemon wedges on the side and a drizzle of olive oil, if desired. Enjoy this comforting and flavorful Middle Eastern classic.

tips: For a richer flavor, consider adding a bay leaf to the simmering soup and removing it before blending.
A splash of good quality extra virgin olive oil drizzled over the top just before serving enhances the authentic taste.
Store leftover Adas in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. It thickens as it cools, so you may need to add a little water or broth when reheating.
article: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is a beloved Middle Eastern Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
